name: Commodity basic description
caption: Description of the goods
heading: Commodity

legacySource.note: This is the SHARED 'basic description' page (consignmentCommodityAttributes.html), rendered for CHED-P (CVEDP) and CHED-D (CED) via the isChedD/isChedp branches, and the CHED-PP variant lives in a separate template (consignmentCommodityAttributesChedpp.html). For CHED-D the handler sets isChedD=true, which selects the addCommodity radios with the 'same country of origin' hint. Type/class/family selects and the species checkbox group are NOT gated by CHED type — they render whenever the selected commodity code yields more than one type/class/family option or any species (handler getViewModel + template conditionals). So for some CHED-D commodity codes these controls DO appear; the two evidence traces used a commodity code (10064000 Broken rice) that produced none of them, which is why no trace rendered them.

structure.text: Commodity | Commodity code / Description | 10064000 / Broken rice
Do you want to add another commodity? / (hint) You can only add another commodity if it has the same country of origin. If not, create a separate notification. / Yes / No
Type of commodity (conditional — only when the commodity code yields >1 type option; hidden input otherwise)
structure.evidence: consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:50-67 (label 'Type of commodity', id type-select, name type); handler getViewModel typeOptions consignment_commodity_attributes.js:300-301. Not rendered in either evidence trace (Broken rice had a single/empty type option).

structure.text: Class of commodity (conditional — only when >1 class option)

structure.text: Family of commodity (conditional — only when >1 family option)

structure.text: {notification.type} Select species of commodity / (hint) Select all that apply. / one checkbox per species (conditional — only when the commodity code yields species)

govukComponents.usage: 'Do you want to add another commodity?' Yes/No decision governing whether a further commodity is added to this notification

govukComponents.usage: Wraps the addCommodity radio group; legend is the medium-weight question. A second fieldset (id species-list) wraps the conditional species checkbox group when species options exist.

trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 19; corroborated page object CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:34-36 (btnSaveAndContinue, getByRole button 'Save and continue') and workflow ched-d-workflows.ts:212; legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:209

govukComponents.usage: Conditional Type/Class/Family of commodity dropdowns. Rendered only when the selected commodity code yields more than one option at that hierarchy level; otherwise a hidden input carries the single value. Never rendered in the CHED-D evidence traces (Broken rice had none).
Conditional species selection ('Select all that apply.'). Rendered only when the selected commodity code yields species. Never rendered in the CHED-D evidence traces.
'Back' link to the previous page (consignment/page-3). Rendered in the service-information region above main.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 19 (a11y tree: link 'Back' -> /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057/consignment/page-3); corroborated legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:5 (backLink to .../consignment/page-3)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom (non-govuk) class applied to a wrapper around the commodity govuk-table, presumably to add horizontal scroll / responsive behaviour on narrow viewports.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not part of the GOV.UK Design System. The DS Table component has no built-in responsive-scroll variant, so IPAFFS added a bespoke class. Minor, but the new app should decide deliberately how to make the commodity table responsive rather than copy this class.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Table wrapped in a container with the standard overflow-x auto pattern (no DS component provides this natively).
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 19 (class inventory: 'table-responsive' is the only non-govuk- class in main); corroborated legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:24 (<div class="table-responsive">)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom (non-govuk) class styling a submit <button> to look like a link. Used for the 'Add another commodity' / 'Remove' actions in the non-CHED-D branches; not present for CHED-D (the isChedD branch suppresses the family 'Add another commodity' button in favour of the addCommodity radios).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke link-styled button outside the DS. Not a CHED-D concern (suppressed for CHED-D) but noted for completeness of the shared template.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use the govuk radios decision (as CHED-D already does) rather than a link-styled submit button.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:210-212 ({{#unless (or isChedpp isChedp isChedD)}} ... link-button 'Add another commodity')

fields.name: addCommodity
fields.label: Do you want to add another commodity?
fields.hint: You can only add another commodity if it has the same country of origin. If not, create a separate notification.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — REQUIRED for CHED-D. Validator adds a required Joi rule for addCommodity when viewModel.addCommodity is undefined and the type is CVEDP, CHEDP or isChedD: consignment_commodity_attributes.js (validator) lines 40-47. The HTML 'required' attribute is not set on the inputs (validation is server-side), consistent with the rendered trace. requiredness here is the legacy policy for CHED-D; the rebuild may revisit whether a Yes/No add-another decision must be a hard-required field.
fields.validationMessage: Select yes if you want to add another commodity
fields.hintEvidence: legacy — hint copy is the constant SINGLE_JOURNEY_CHEDD_COMMODITY_HINT (constants.js:971), passed as chedDHint only in the isChedD branch; exact string match to the rendered trace.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 19; corroborated b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 17. Radio values: Yes=true (id addCommodity), No=false (id addCommodity-2). Legacy: template consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:187-208 (govuk/radios name addCommodity, isChedD branch with chedDHint), handler addAnotherCommodityValues consignment_commodity_attributes.js:272. Selecting 'true' routes to the Search Commodity page to add another (CONSIGNMENT_SELECT), getNextPage consignment_commodity_attributes.js:193-195. Page object corroboration: CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:18-20 exposes ONLY radioNo; workflow drives radioNo.check() at ched-d-workflows.ts:211; a11y test ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:42. The single commodity in the read-only table (10064000 / Broken rice) is the DEFAULT_CONFIG commodityCode (commodityCode.brokenRice, commodity-code.ts:6), selected on the preceding Search Commodity page (ched-d-workflows.ts:207-208).

fields.name: typeOfCommodity
fields.label: Type of commodity
fields.conditional: Rendered only when the selected commodity code yields more than one type option; when a single option exists it is submitted via a hidden input (name=type) with no visible control.
options: (dynamic — from the reference-data commodity category 'types' list for the selected commodity code)
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — the model requires a species/type hierarchy for CED; when multiple type options exist the user must pick one (the select is not optional). Mandatoriness is implicit in the hierarchy walk (getViewModel resets class/family when type changes) rather than a distinct Joi rule for CHED-D. Rebuild should confirm whether it wants this hierarchy at all.
fields.evidence: legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:50-67 (label 'Type of commodity', id type-select, name type); handler getViewModel typeOptions/typeOptionSelected consignment_commodity_attributes.js:300-301. Corroborated page object dropdownTypeOfCommodity (getByLabel 'Type of commodity') CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:6-8 — the CHED-D workflow simply never exercises it because Broken rice has no multi-option hierarchy. NOT rendered in either evidence trace.

fields.name: classOfCommodity
fields.label: Class of commodity
fields.conditional: Rendered only when >1 class option for the selected type; single option submitted via hidden input (name=class).
options: (dynamic — commodity category 'classes' filtered by selected type)
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — part of the commodity type/class/family hierarchy; a class must be chosen when multiple exist. Same implicit-mandatoriness caveat as typeOfCommodity.
fields.evidence: legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:75-92 (label 'Class of commodity', id class-select, name class); handler classOptions consignment_commodity_attributes.js:302-303, getClassesFrom line 333-335. NOT rendered in either evidence trace.

fields.name: familyOfCommodity
fields.label: Family of commodity
fields.conditional: Rendered only when >1 family option for the selected class; single option submitted via hidden input (name=family).
options: (dynamic — commodity category 'families' filtered by selected class, excluding blank text)
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — part of the type/class/family hierarchy; a family must be chosen when multiple exist. Same implicit-mandatoriness caveat.
fields.evidence: legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:100-117 (label 'Family of commodity', id family-select, name family); handler familyOptions consignment_commodity_attributes.js:304-305, getFamiliesFromWithFilter line 337-339. NOT rendered in either evidence trace.

fields.name: species
fields.label: {notification.type} Select species of commodity
fields.conditional: Rendered only when the selected commodity code (and chosen class/family) yields species; when a single empty species exists it is submitted via a hidden input (name=species). Multiple checkboxes named 'species'.
options: (dynamic — species list for the selected commodity, one checkbox per species; when species carry EPPO codes they are shown in a table with Genus (and Species) / EPPO code columns)
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — REQUIRED when species options exist. Validator adds a required Joi rule for species when viewModel.species.length > 0: consignment_commodity_attributes.js (validator) lines 32-38. Error message SPECIES for non-CHEDPP. This is conditional-required (only when the commodity has species); the rebuild should confirm which CHED-D commodities carry a species list.
fields.evidence: legacy consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:123-183 (fieldset id species-list, legend prefixed with notification.type + 'Select species of commodity', hint id species-hint 'Select all that apply.', checkbox name species); handler getSpeciesFrom consignment_commodity_attributes.js:306, 341-352; markSelectedSpecies lines 354-362. On a validation error the field key is remapped species -> species-list (handler handleValidationError lines 173-175). Corroborated page object checkboxSpecies/checkboxChedPPSpecies CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:10-16 — unexercised by the CHED-D workflow because Broken rice yields no species. NOT rendered in either evidence trace.

validationMessages.message: Select yes if you want to add another commodity
validationMessages.trigger: Notifier submits without choosing Yes or No for 'Do you want to add another commodity?' (CHED-D always requires the decision).
validationMessages.evidence: en.js:42 (ADD_COMMODITY); wired in validator consignment_commodity_attributes.js lines 40-47 (required for isChedD / CVEDP / CHEDP). This resolves openQuestion #2 — the previously-uncaptured required-error copy is now sourced from legacy.

validationMessages.trigger: Commodity has species options and the notifier submits without ticking any species checkbox (non-CHED-PP). For CHED-PP the message is 'Select at least one Genus (and Species)'.
validationMessages.evidence: en.js:141 (SPECIES); wired in validator consignment_commodity_attributes.js lines 32-38 (required when viewModel.species.length > 0).

validationMessages.message: Commodity lines must be fewer than {maxCommodityLines + 1}
validationMessages.trigger: Adding this commodity/species would push the notification over the configured maximum number of commodity lines (non-CHED-PP wording; CHED-PP uses 'Select a maximum of {N} genus and species').
validationMessages.evidence: en.js:106 (MAX_COMMODITY_LINES, interpolates config.maxCommodityLines constants.js:12) and en.js:107 (MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_REACHED for CHED-PP); wired in validator consignment_commodity_attributes.js exceedsMaxCommodityLines rule.

validationMessages.trigger: Model-level (notification-microservice) validation on submit if no commodity complement is present for the consignment. Backs the frontend selection on the preceding Search Commodity page; included here because this is where the commodity/species selection is finalised.
validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:91 (uk.gov.defra.tracesx.notificationschema.representation.partone.euimp.commodities.commodityComplement.min.message). Commodities.java @NotNull commodityComplement for CED group NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation.class (Commodities.java:189-201 group set) — CED = CHED-D.

discrepancies: openQuestion #3 corrected by legacy: the Type of commodity dropdown, Class/Family selects and species checkboxes exposed by the shared page object are NOT exclusive to CHED-A/CHED-P/CHED-PP. The template's isChedD branch renders the SAME type/class/family/species controls (consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:46-183); the handler builds typeOptions/classOptions/familyOptions/species for every type (consignment_commodity_attributes.js:300-306). They are gated by whether the selected commodity code produces >1 option (or any species), not by CHED type. The two evidence traces used Broken rice (10064000), which produced a single-option hierarchy and no species, so none rendered — but for other CHED-D commodity codes these ARE CHED-D fields. They have been promoted from 'excluded other-CHED controls' to conditional legacy fields.

openQuestion #4 partly resolved by legacy: the page <title> 'Select species of commodity' is NOT a stale carryover unique to a species page — it is the CONSIGNMENT_TITLE constant hard-set for the whole shared template for every CHED type including CHED-D (consignment_commodity_attributes.js:3 and :258, pageTitle: CONSIGNMENT_TITLE). It genuinely mismatches the H1 'Commodity' in the real system too. Rendered value kept (confirmed); the mismatch is a real IPAFFS quirk the rebuild should fix, not a trace artefact.

The species fieldset legend in legacy is prefixed with the notification type token: '{notification.type} Select species of commodity' (consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:129-132). No trace rendered the species branch, so the exact runtime prefix for CHED-D (e.g. 'CED' vs 'CHEDD') is a residual gap for a human to confirm.
openQuestions: The 'Yes' branch (addCommodity=true, add another commodity of the same country of origin) is never exercised in the two evidence traces (both select 'No', a single commodity) and is never driven by any CHED-D test. Legacy confirms the flow: submitting addCommodity=true routes to the Search Commodity page (CONSIGNMENT_SELECT) to add a further commodity (getNextPage consignment_commodity_attributes.js:193-195), which loops back to this page. The page object exposes no radioYes getter, so the multi-commodity loop is untested but its wiring is now legacy-sourced.

RESOLVED by legacy (was: no validation copy). The required-error copy for addCommodity is 'Select yes if you want to add another commodity' (en.js:42), and the conditional species error is 'Select at least one species' (en.js:141). Both are now in validationMessages. What is still a gap: whether the rebuild wants the same hard-required addCommodity policy for CHED-D, and which CHED-D commodity codes actually carry a species list (the traces used one that does not).

RESOLVED/PROMOTED by legacy (was: shared page object exposes other-CHED controls). dropdownTypeOfCommodity, checkboxSpecies, class/family selects are rendered by the CHED-D branch of the shared template when the commodity hierarchy has multiple options / species — see discrepancies[0]. They are now conditional legacy fields, not excluded controls. inputEppoCode (CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:22-24), btnSearch and btnAddSpecies belong to the CHED-PP-specific template (consignmentCommodityAttributesChedpp.html) and remain out of CHED-D scope.

The page <title> 'Select species of commodity' mismatches the H1 'Commodity' and the slug — confirmed to be a hard-set constant for the whole shared template (see discrepancies[1]). The new app should set an accurate page title.

The observed URL is .../consignment/page-4 with a query string commodity-selected-code=10064000 (IPAFFS uses numbered pages, not the semantic slug). Legacy confirms formAction 'page-4' and the hidden commodity-selected-code input (consignment_commodity_attributes.js:268; consignmentCommodityAttributes.html:43). The slug 'commodity-basic-description' is the exercise's own naming.

The commodity summary table shows the commodity(ies) already selected on the preceding Search Commodity page (both traces: 10064000 / Broken rice). This page itself does not capture the commodity code — it only shows the running list, asks whether to add another, and (for some codes) captures type/class/family/species. The commodity code selection lives on the preceding Search Commodity page (ched-d-workflows.ts:207-208).
